license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qjmduk6
title: Fluorescence microscopy with the marine heterotrophic flagellate Cafeteria roenbergensis
author: Monica Berjon-Otero

steps_raw.instruction: Follow the protocol: " Lysozyme-based removal of bacteria from cultures of the marine heterotrophic flagellate Cafeteria roenbergensis " If the culture is to be treated with other reagents, for example L-Azidohomoalanine, dilute the cells without bacteria to the required density and apply the treatment.
Aliquot 5 x 10 6 Cafeteria cells per 1.5 ml microfuge tube for each microscopy sample
Centrifuge for 5 min at 5,000 g, 20ºC
Resuspend each pellet in 500 µL of 4% paraformaldehyde (PFA) in 1X PBS
Incubate for 20 min at 20ºC

Centrifuge for 5 min at 7,000 g, 20ºC You can keep the sample overnight at 4 ºC by resuspending the pellet with 250 µL 1X PBS On the following day, centrifuge for 5 min at 5,000 g, 20ºC and continue with the next step
Resuspend the pellets in 500 µL of 0.5% Triton in 1X PBS
Incubate for 15 min at 20ºC
Clean the filter-support grids of a vacuum manifold system with pure water (e.g. ELGA, milliQ)
Apply vaccum to remove the drop of water
Add a drop of ELGA water on each grid of the vacuum system that you want to use and place a 0.45 µm Millipore support filter on top of it
Add another drop of ELGA water on top of the Millipore filter
Place a 0.2 µm, 25 mm Anodisc Whatman filter on top of the ELGA water drop
Apply vacuum with a pressure between 5-10 Hg to remove the water
If neccesary, block or wash the filter with the required solution. Remove the solution by applying vacuum
Place the Cafeteria sample from step 8 on top of the Whatman filter

Apply vacuum with a pressure between 5-10 Hg to remove the permeabilization buffer and to immobilize the cells on the filter Perform all the neccesary blocking and washing steps following the same instructions
Place the filter with the Cafeteria sample on a piece of Parafilm. Add the staining solution of interest on top of the filter and incubate following the manufacturer's instructions
Place the filter on the vacuum system again
Apply vacuum with a pressure between 5-10 Hg to remove the remaining staining buffer
If required, wash the filter again with the buffer of interest
If desired, stain DNA by incubating with an appropriate reagent (DAPI, SYBR, Hoechst)
Apply vacuum with a pressure between 5-10 Hg to remove the remaining liquid
Add 10 µL of 70% glycerol on a glass slide and place the filter on top of the glycerol The filter must be placed with the Cafeteria cells in the top!
Add 10 µL of 70% glycerol on the coverslip and place it on top of the filter
Apply pressure on the sample during 20 min at room temperature
Seal the coverslip with nail polish Samples can be kept at 4 °C or analyzed with the microscope immediatly
